x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. NYPL is the counter-example: it already operates real public APIs (Digital Collections, Locations, Research Catalog). This artifact unifies them into one agent-native contract and adds place_hold — the net-new write workflow that today has no clean, documented public API.

server.name: io.nyc.nypl
server.title: New York Public Library (NYPL)
server.description: Agent-native access to the New York Public Library — search Digital Collections, find branches and their hours/amenities/events, and search the Research Catalog, all over NYPL's already-real public APIs unified into one contract, plus the net-new ability to place and track a hold (reservation) on a catalog item.
server.instructions: Use search_digital_collections / get_digital_item to explore digitized items (set publicDomainOnly for reusable images); list_collections for curated groupings. find_branches / get_branch return NYPL locations with hours, amenities, and accessibility (NYPL serves Manhattan, the Bronx, and Staten Island; Brooklyn and Queens have separate systems). find_events and get_branch_events cover public programs and exhibitions. search_catalog / get_catalog_item query the Research and circulating catalog; only circulating, holdable copies can be reserved. list_my_holds and place_hold require an authenticated patron and never expose other patrons' data — place_hold mutates state and needs patron eligibility, so confirm the exact item (bibId) and pickup branch with the user before submitting.

tools.name: search_digital_collections
tools.title: Search Digital Collections
tools.description: Search NYPL Digital Collections digitized items by free text, research division, and public-domain status.

tools.name: get_digital_item
tools.title: Get Digital Item
tools.description: Fetch a NYPL Digital Collections item (metadata, captures, rights) by UUID.

tools.name: list_collections
tools.title: List Collections
tools.description: List or search NYPL Digital Collections collections.

tools.name: find_branches
tools.title: Find Branches
tools.description: List NYPL locations (branches and research centers) by borough, type, and open-now status, with hours and amenities.

tools.name: get_branch
tools.title: Get Branch
tools.description: Fetch one NYPL location (hours, amenities, access, contacts, links) by slug.

tools.name: get_branch_events
tools.title: Get Branch Events
tools.description: List upcoming public programs and exhibitions at a specific NYPL location.

tools.name: find_events
tools.title: Find Events
tools.description: Search NYPL public programs, classes, and exhibitions across locations by text, branch, type, audience, or date range.

tools.name: search_catalog
tools.title: Search Catalog
tools.description: Search the NYPL Research and circulating catalog (books, ebooks, film, and more), filtering by material type, language, collection, and holdability.

tools.name: get_catalog_item
tools.title: Get Catalog Item
tools.description: Fetch a NYPL catalog bibliographic record and its copies/availability by bib id.

tools.name: list_my_holds
tools.title: List My Holds
tools.description: List the authenticated patron's own holds/reservations and their status — today reachable only inside the patron's Research Catalog account.

tools.name: place_hold
tools.title: Place a Hold
tools.description: Place a hold (reservation) on a NYPL catalog item for pickup at a branch. NET-NEW agent-native workflow — NYPL runs the internal hold/eligibility services but has no clean public hold API. Requires an authenticated, eligible patron. Mutates state — confirm the item (bibId) and pickup branch first.
